member.name: Euan Stainbank
member.synopsis: Euan Stainbank is the Labour MP for Falkirk , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Data Centres: Environment Protection
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what plans her Department has to publish national guidance on the environmental impacts of AI data centres.
writtenQuestions.answerText: DSIT engages regularly with DEFRA, the Environment Agency and DESNZ, to discuss the impact of AI data centres on the environment. Through the AI Energy Council, the Government is assessing and promoting energy efficiency and clean power solutions. DSIT wi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Airlines: Takeovers
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether her Department has assessed the adequacy of existing public interest powers to scrutinise potential takeovers of strategically important airlines.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK has a robust framework for scrutinising acquisitions where relevant public interest or national security concerns arise. Existing powers, including the National Security and Investment Act 2021, enable Government scrutiny and intervention where sta...

writtenQuestions.heading: Neonicotinoids
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps her Department is taking to publish a timetable for ending emergency authorisations of neonicotinoid pesticides; and what discussions she has had with the Scottish Governmen
writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government has already taken decisive action on neonicotinoids: it set out plans to change existing policies to prevent the use of three neonicotinoid pesticides that threaten vital pollinators, including through emergency authorisations, in our poli...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Individual Savings Accounts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, whether she will exempt Lifetime ISA account holders from the withdrawal charge that can no longer purchase a house due to a significant change of personal circumstances.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Lifetime ISA is intended to support saving for a first home or later life, and the withdrawal charge helps ensure that Government support is used for those purposes. Introducing exemptions based on individual circumstances would undermine the LISA’s o...

writtenQuestions.heading: Buses: Electric Vehicles
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether she is planning to consult with the bus manufacturing and operating sector on timescales for the Zero-Emission Bus mandate.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Bus Services Act 2025 includes a measure to accelerate decarbonisation of bus services, by placing a requirement on bus operators ultimately not to use new non-zero emission buses (ZEBs) on local bus routes in England.The date from which new non ZEBs ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Hospitality Industry: VAT
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, how many and what proportion of hospitality businesses in Scotland are beneath the VAT registration threshold.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The information requested is not available, as the registered addresses of firms held in HMRC’s datasets does not always correspond to the firms’ places of business, and in many cases firms operate both in Scotland and elsewhere in the UK. The UK has the ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Hospitality Industry: VAT
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the the potential merits of changing the VAT registration threshold for hospitality businesses.
writtenQuestions.answerText: At £90,000, the UK has the second highest registration threshold among all 38 OECD countries, which keeps the majority of businesses outside the VAT system. VAT is a broad-based tax on consumption, and the 20 per cent standard rate applies to most goods a...

writtenQuestions.heading: Hospitality Industry: VAT
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, whether she has made an estimate of the potential impact of reducing VAT to 10 per cent for food and beverage hospitality businesses on revenue to her Department.
writtenQuestions.answerText: At £90,000, the UK has the second highest registration threshold among all 38 OECD countries, which keeps the majority of businesses outside the VAT system. VAT is a broad-based tax on consumption, and the 20 per cent standard rate applies to most goods a...

writtenQuestions.heading: Animal Products: Labelling
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps her Department is taking to develop plans for a mandatory method of production labelling for animal products.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the reply given to the hon. Member for Putney, Fleur Anderson, on 29 January 2026, PQ UIN 106592.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Cuba: Humanitarian Aid
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what recent discussions she has had with international partners on improving humanitarian access to Cuba.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the responses I gave in the Urgent Question debate on 8 June.

writtenQuestions.heading: Women: Public Places
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for Women and Equalities, with reference to 13.167 of the Code of Practice for Services, Public Functions and Associations, if she will provide a list of the identity documents that can be requested by service providers seeking to conf
writtenQuestions.answerText: The draft Code does not specify a list of identity documents. It makes clear that providers should clearly communicate their access policies, for example through signage.

writtenQuestions.heading: Housing: Timber
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what assessment he has made of the efficacy of the planning system to encourage the use of domestic timber in house building.
writtenQuestions.answerText: My Department has made no such specific assessment.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Armed Forces: Housing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what steps he is taking to maximise the use of domestically manufactured building materials for the construction of service homes.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Defence is committed to maximising the use of domestically manufactured building materials in the construction and refurbishment of Defence family homes where this delivers value for money, supports supply chain resilience, and is complian...

writtenQuestions.heading: Data Centres: Scotland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, how many data centre projects in Scotland are currently seeking electricity grid connections; and what potential aggregate capacity in gigawatts those applications represent.
writtenQuestions.answerText: National Energy System Operator shows around 72.8 GW of capacity allocated to data centres in the GB connection queue across transmission and distribution. It is not currently possible to provide a specific figure for data centre projects in Scotland. Gov...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Data Centres: Electricity
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what proportion of electricity demand connection applications in the National Energy System Operator queue are attributable to data centres.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Data supplied by the National Energy System Operator shows around 60GW of capacity allocated to data centres in the transmission queue. This represents around three quarters of the total demand connections queue, at transmission level. Government recently...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Data Centres: Electricity
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, if he will publish the National Energy System Operator's analysis of future electricity demand from data centres.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The National Energy System Operator (NESO) is currently working towards releasing its latest 10 Year Forecast on 30th July. This will include NESO’s estimates of the annual electricity demand associated with data centres. It will be published on the NESO ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Refineries: Grangemouth
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what is the mean average individual award made under the Grangemouth Training Guarantee.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK and Scottish Governments are investing up to £2 million to help workers directly impacted by the cessation of refining at Grangemouth transition into new jobs. The Grangemouth Training Guarantee has already supported over 290 workers through traini...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Refineries: Grangemouth
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what is the maximum individual award under the Grangemouth Training Guarantee.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK and Scottish Governments are investing up to £2 million to help workers directly impacted by the cessation of refining at Grangemouth transition into new jobs. The Grangemouth Training Guarantee has already supported over 290 workers through traini...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Workplace Pensions: Discounts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she made of the potential merits of an immediate implementation of the SCAPE discount rate.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government remains committed to reviewing the SCAPE discount rate at every valuation cycle. The SCAPE discount rate was announced in Parliament on 19 May 2026. This is in line with established precedent for reviews of the SCAPE discount rate. Where th...

writtenQuestions.heading: Police: Workplace Pensions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of an increase in the SCAPE discount rate on police officers in the 1987 Police Pension Scheme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In line with the existing methodology, the Government announced on 19 May 2026 that the SCAPE discount rate is 2%+CPI. HM Treasury is not the responsible authority for individual Public Service Pension Schemes. Regulation B7 of the Police Pension Scheme R...
